items.title: Hyperparameter grids for tree depth and polynomial degree
items.description: Production ML pipelines grid-search max_depth, min_samples_leaf, or polynomial degree with cross-validation. The winning value is the complexity setting at the validation minimum — not the setting with lowest training loss.

items.title: Neural architecture search and scaling
items.description: Teams sweep width, depth, and layer types until validation metrics plateau. Under-sized nets underfit; oversized nets on small data overfit — architecture is the complexity choice for deep learning.

items.title: AutoML and complexity-aware model selection
items.description: AutoML tools try model families with different capacities (linear, boosted trees, small nets) and stop at validation-optimal complexity, often preferring simpler models within an error tolerance.

items.title: Regularization as complexity control in production
items.description: Ridge alpha, L1 strength, dropout, and early stopping dial effective complexity without changing architecture — common when nominal capacity must stay high but data is limited.

items.title: A/B testing simpler vs richer models
items.description: Ship the least complex model whose offline validation is within business tolerance of the best score — simpler models reduce latency, cost, and variance-driven regressions when data drifts.
